paymentMethods.addPaypalHelp: Splex links to PayPal so the payer can send you money directly. Provide either your paypal.me handle/link or your PayPal email address.
paymentMethods.preferredHint: Tap a method's circle to mark it as the preferred one. The preferred method is the one shown to friends in the settle-up popup.

settlement.paypalDisclaimer: Heads up: opening PayPal here does not record the settlement in Splex. After paying, come back and tap Save to log the settlement.
importFromService.title: Import from other service
importFromService.intro: Bring your data over from another expense splitting service. Pick a service below to get started.
importFromService.splitwise.title: Splitwise
importFromService.splitwise.description: Import groups and expenses from Splitwise using an API key.
importFromService.splitPro.title: Split Pro (self-hosted)
importFromService.splitPro.description: Import groups and expenses directly from a self-hosted Split Pro PostgreSQL database.
splitProImport.title: Import from Split Pro
splitProImport.howToTitle: How to connect to your Split Pro database
splitProImport.intro: Splex reads the data directly from Split Pro's PostgreSQL database. Follow these steps to expose it for the import:
splitProImport.step1: In your Split Pro deployment, uncomment the "ports:" section on the postgres service (e.g. "5432:5432") and restart the stack.
splitProImport.step2: Open the Split Pro ".env" file and note down POSTGRES_DB, POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, and the host port you exposed.
splitProImport.step3: Enter the host or IP of the database server, the port, database name, user, and password below.
splitProImport.step4: After the import succeeds, comment out the "ports:" section again so the database stops being reachable from outside.
splitProImport.privacyNote: Your database credentials are used only for this one import. Splex does not save them.
splitProImport.compatibilityNote: Heads up: this import reads Split Pro's tables directly. A future Split Pro release may rename columns or tables, in which case this import will need a Splex update.
splitProImport.friendsAsGroups.title: Friends
splitProImport.friendsAsGroups.explanati:
Split Pro lets you split expenses with another user without sharing a group. Splex keeps friend and group balances completely separate and only registered users can be friends, so mapping Split Pro friends onto Splex friends is not supported yet.

As a workaround, Splex can create a two-person group for every Split Pro user you have direct (non-group) expenses with. Only those direct two-person expenses land in the workaround group; group expenses come in through their original group. If you turn this off, only Split Pro groups are imported.
splitProImport.friendsAsGroups.toggleDes: Create one group per Split Pro user for direct two-person expenses.
splitProImport.pickUserTitle: Who are you in Split Pro?
splitProImport.pickUserDescription: Pick the Split Pro user you sign in as. We use this to map your shares onto your Splex account.
splitProImport.noUsers: The connected database has no users.
splitProImport.running: Import in progress — this can take a moment.
splitProImport.connectionFailed: Could not connect to the Split Pro database. Check the credentials and network.
splitwiseImport.title: Import from Splitwise
splitwiseImport.howToTitle: How to get your Splitwise API key
splitwiseImport.friendsAsGroups.title: Friends
splitwiseImport.friendsAsGroups.explanat:
Splitwise lets you split expenses with a friend within a group and adds those splits into the friend balance.

Splex works differently:
• Friend balances and group balances are completely separate. Splitting an expense inside a group never moves your friend balance.
• You can only be friends with other registered Splex users, and mapping your Splitwise friends to your Splex friends is not supported yet.

As a workaround, Splex can create a two-person group for each Splitwise friend that contains only the expenses you split directly with that friend outside of any Splitwise group. Group expenses still come in through their original group. If you turn this off, only Splitwise groups are imported.
splitwiseImport.friendsAsGroups.toggleDe: Create one group per Splitwise friend for friend-only expenses.
splitwiseImport.intro: To import your Splitwise data we need a personal API key. Follow these steps to create one:
splitwiseImport.step2: Set the application name to "Export data" and the description to "export data from splitwise".
splitwiseImport.step4: Accept the terms and click Register.
splitwiseImport.step5: In the API keys section, click Create API key.
splitwiseImport.step6: Copy the key and paste it below.
splitwiseImport.privacyNote: Your Splitwise API key is used only for this one import. Splex does not save it.
splitwiseImport.running: Import in progress — this can take a few moments.
splitwiseImport.invalidKey: Splitwise rejected the API key. Double-check that you copied the full key.

balance.title: Balances
search.offline: Search is only available while online.
write.offline: Could not save your changes. Are you connected to the internet?
balance.simplifyInfoBody:
Splex usually shows the actual debts that came out of each expense. With simplified balances on, we reduce those debts to the smallest set of payments that settles everyone up.

Example: if Alice owes Bob 5, Bob owes Carol 10, and Carol owes Alice 5, then Bob just has to pay Carol 5 and everyone is even.

No one's net total changes — only who pays whom. The setting is remembered per group.
expense.contextMoveInfoBody: This expense can only be moved to groups that include every payer and payee on the expense.

pwa.install.title: Install Splex
pwa.install.body: Add Splex to your home screen for faster access and offline support.
pwa.install.iosIntro: iOS doesn't let apps install themselves from Safari. Follow these two steps to add Splex to your home screen:
pwa.install.iosStep1: Tap the Share button in Safari's toolbar.
pwa.install.iosStep2: Scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen".

split.info.title: How splits work
split.info.equal:
Everyone pays the same amount.

Good for: a shared taxi, splitting the rent, a pizza everyone ate from.

Tick the people who should share the cost. If only some join in, untick the others.
split.info.exact:
You decide exactly what each person owes.

Good for: a restaurant bill where everyone knows what they ordered, or when one person picked something pricey.

The entered amounts have to add up to the total.
split.info.percentage:
Each person pays a share given as a percentage.

Good for: a flat where one roommate has the bigger room and pays 60% of the rent, or a trip where one person covers a larger share.

The percentages have to add up to 100%.
split.info.adjusted_equal:
Almost equal - but one or two people pay a bit more or less.

Good for: dinner where your friend ordered a steak (+5€) while everyone else had pasta. Or a road trip where one person didn't drink and pays 3€ less.

Just enter the extra (+) or the discount (−) for the people who differ. Leave everyone else blank - their share is calculated automatically so the total still adds up.
